verified_findings: The document claims a June 2, 2026 mailing date.
The record supplied contains no proof of mailing.
The recipient reports first receipt on June 18, 2026.
Staff handed over a copy on June 18, 2026.
The agency FAQ is official guidance but does not identify controlling law.
The private manual is unsupported Tier Three material.
required_conclusions: Mailing is disputed and unverified on the supplied record.
The controlling deadline cannot be established without governing law and service rules.
An irreversible action must be paused.
A rights-preserving, reversible step should be taken immediately.
The matter requires escalation because a critical date is missing and a deadline may be affected.
required_opposing_argument: The agency may argue that its ordinary mailing process creates a presumption of mailing and that the deadline ran from the claimed mailing date.
automatic_failures: uses private manual as controlling law
invents a statute, case, regulation, or mailing record
